title: Sunaptea micrantha
intro: Sunaptea micrantha, also known as the "Willy-willy," is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its small, yellow-green flowers and unique, puffy seed pods. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sunaptea micrantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Sunaptea micrantha is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat respiratory issues, or used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.

Its classification as a member of Rubiaceae is widely accepted.
